name: Two-Sided Marketplace
description: Clean, trustworthy marketplace serving both buyers and sellers. Listing browser, buyer and seller dashboards, messaging, reviews, and full auth with marketing landing.
personality: Clean, trustworthy marketplace that serves both sides fairly. White/light surfaces with a single accent color for CTAs. Listing cards are image-forward with clean typography. Search and filtering are powerful but never overwhelming. Reviews are prominent — social proof drives conversion. Messaging is simple and contextual. Seller dashboard is data-rich but not intimidating. Comparison tools help buyers decide. Lucide icons. Mobile-first thinking.

voice.tone: Friendly and trustworthy. Speaks to both buyers and sellers.
voice.empty_states: Encouraging and helpful. Empty listings invite sellers to create their first. Empty favorites encourage browsing. Always warm.
voice.errors: Friendly and reassuring. Never lose user progress. Offer clear recovery steps.
voice.loading: Image-forward skeleton cards for listings. Smooth progressive loading. Seller dashboard shows shimmer tables.

directives: Domain vocabulary: this blueprint scaffolds RENTAL marketplaces (cabins, studios, vehicles, gear) — NOT workshop/class/session marketplaces. Booking calendars MUST be date-range / nightly oriented. Avoid copy that implies pre-scheduled sessions ('Workshop tour 2-5p', 'Brunch + walk', 'Sunset cookout' as listing labels) — those break the rental immersion. Listings are SPACES and TOOLS lent for spans of time, not hourly classes.

Image strategy: this blueprint is image-forward by personality. Listings, host avatars, and the marketing hero MUST treat image slots as PRIMARY UX. When real images are not seeded, the AI must (a) keep the image area present at correct proportions, (b) render a SUBTLE placeholder (low-saturation themed gradient + tiny category icon centered, OR a soft mesh pattern), and (c) NOT ship raw saturated linear-gradient blocks that dominate the card — those read as broken/unfinished UI.

Marketing hero on /: must include supporting media — at minimum a 'visual stack' to the right of the headline (a small map snippet, 2-3 listing card previews, or an illustration), NOT just headline + subtitle + CTA + sparse stat row. A marketplace landing page with no listing imagery feels empty.

Listing card anatomy: every listing card across browse, search, favorites, and seller-listings MUST surface five anchor fields — image, title, location (city), rating (stars + count), price (with unit). Three fields (title + host + price) reads as MVP-not-shipped.

Comparison-table: do NOT inject on every listing-detail by default. Only render when the page can show 2-3 actually-comparable spaces. Otherwise it produces awkward 'RECOMMENDED' badges floating over single-column tables.

Voice consistency: lean rental/space/host/stay/review vocabulary. Avoid SaaS-default copy like 'Get started', 'Sign up', 'Submit' — use the declared voice.cta_verbs (Browse, Book, List, Compare, Message, Review).

Default route: `/` is the public marketing landing, NOT the authenticated buyer surface. Once signed in, redirect default to `/browse` (or `/buyer` if seller-mode is off). Never auto-redirect to `/seller/overview` as the default.

Theme switching: this blueprint's personality reads 'clean / trustworthy / light surfaces with a single accent.' BEST FIT themes are clean (default), paper, counsel, lab, launchpad, bistro, liquid-glass — calm light/professional aesthetics. SECONDARY FIT themes (carbon, carbon-neon, prismatic, retro) work but the AI must DAMPEN their ambient effects on rental UX: do not apply orbs/breathing-gradients/scanlines to listing cards or the booking widget — those work on agent/AI surfaces, not on rental-trust surfaces. NOT RECOMMENDED themes (luminarum, neon-cyber, neo-tokyo, terminal, gaming-guild, studio-dark) clash hard with 'trustworthy host' tone — if a user explicitly switches to them, ground the visual language in neutral surfaces and use theme decorators only on accent CTAs / status chips, not on the listing cards themselves.

Theme decorator scope: regardless of theme, listing cards must remain image-forward + readable above all else. A vibrant theme should color the CTA, status chip, and rating star — not the entire card surface. The card surface should stay neutral so the listing image and copy lead. This is what 'image-forward, single-accent' means in practice when themes vary.
